figures.caption: Figure 1: PCA of situation-dependent and global persona neurons, with proximate topic names annotated. Comparable distances between distinct personality domains and topics within same personality domain highlight the crucial impact of situations.

Table 2: Personality performance results on SPBench. Notation is consistent with Table 1.

Table 16: Prompt template used for the intrinsic evaluation of SPBench.
Figure 9: The human evaluation interface. Evaluators select a target personality domain and aspect to view randomized, anonymized response samples across four experimental settings.

Figure 4: Ablation study results: (a) and (b) depict the personality assessment performance across two distinct test sets under different γ settings. (c) and (d) illustrate the impact of varying σ settings across all evaluation metrics.
Figure 5: PCA of situation-dependent and global persona neurons in Llama-3-8B-Instruct, with proximate topic names annotated.

Figure 2: Empirical study results: (a) Layer-wise counts of persona neurons across situational topics (Topic labels abbreviated by omitting "and") (b) Variability in neuron proportions across early, middle, and late layers for different topics. (c) Validation of the situation-behavior consistency patterns in LLM personality.

Figure 6: Overall PCA results across five personality domains of Llama-3-8B-Instruct (a) and Qwen3-8B (b).
Figure 7: Comprehensive layer analysis of the count distribution of situational persona neurons.
